# Golden fixtures

Reference captures used to validate vantablack output once future
roadmap items land. Files in this directory are committed to git so CI
can diff against them deterministically.

## `gemma4-e2b-reference.txt`

Output of Google's `litert-lm` CLI on
`litert-community/gemma-4-E2B-it-litert-lm` from prompt
"Once upon a time" (15 lines, ~2.5 KB). Generated by the user on macOS;
exact CLI flags not recorded — capture again with `--seed`, `--temp`,
`--max-tokens` documented before relying on bit-equal comparison.

**Gate**: Roadmap item 19e (`.litertlm` forward-path validation) is
declared passing when `vantablack prompt
~/.cache/.../gemma-4-E2B-it.litertlm 256 "Once upon a time"` produces
output that matches this file token-for-token (after stripping markdown
emphasis if Google emits it differently).

Blocked on:
- 19c: TFLite tensor → vantablack `Model` mapping.
- 19d: TFLite int2 / int4 / fp32 dequant + matmul kernels (this model
  uses INT2 for the text embedder and INT4 for the per-layer embedder
  — no INT8 in this specific build).

## `gemma4-e2b-inspect.txt`

Snapshot of `vantablack inspect <model.litertlm>` on the same Gemma 4
E2B file. Documents the actual bundle layout:

- 12 sections total
- 1.2 GB INT4 `per_layer_embedder` table (35 × 32 MB shards)
- 99 MB INT2 text embedder
- Audio conformer + adapter sections (multimodal Gemma 4)
- 780 MB section 10 — likely the transformer decoder block (needs
  per-section deep inspection)
- 42 MB section 11 — LM head candidate

Used as a reference when planning the tensor-name mapping in Phase 19c.
Re-generate with:

```bash
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast
./zig-out/bin/vantablack inspect ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/litertlm-models/gemma-4-E2B-it.litertlm \
  > tests/golden/gemma4-e2b-inspect.txt
```
